era_context: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's reign in Assyria. The Levantine states remain under pressure from Assyrian expansion. In Greece, the Archaic period continues with gradual development of the polis system. Western Zhou in China is approaching its final collapse. Egypt remains fragmented.

events.title: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III continued his annual military campaigns into the Levant, maintaining Assyrian tributary control over the region. The pattern of these campaigns, documented in royal inscriptions, involved systematic subjugation and tribute extraction from local kingdoms including Israel, Damascus, and Phoenician cities. These campaigns established the template for Assyrian imperial expansion that would dominate the Near East for the next century.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns are well-attested; the specific year assignment follows Assyrian chronology with ±2 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events of 818 BCE are individually documented in surviving sources. The year is reconstructed from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign. Some scholars debate the exact chronology of his campaigns, with variations of ±2–3 years in different chronological systems.
historiographic_note: 818 BCE is essentially unattested as a discrete historical moment. The year is assigned significance only through its position within Shalmaneser III's documented reign. This illustrates a fundamental problem with year-by-year history in the ancient world: most years leave no individual trace. The historical record for this period is dominated by Assyrian royal propaganda; other perspectives are largely absent.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same sustained campaign pattern.
